title: Fluid property estimation based on electromagnetic measurements
abstract: A method of a method of analyzing a fluid includes measuring a conductive flow of a fluid circulating through a borehole during a subterranean operation, the conductive flow indicative of an electrically conductive constituent of the fluid. The method also includes measuring a total flow of the fluid concurrently with the measuring of the conductive flow, and estimating a property of the fluid in real time during the subterranean operation based on the conductive flow and the total flow.
claims:
Claims What is claimed: 1. A method (60) of analyzing a fluid (24), characterized by: measuring a conductive flow of a fluid circulating through a borehole (14) during a subterranean operation (16), the conductive flow indicative of an electrically conductive constituent of the fluid (24); measuring a total flow of the fluid (24) concurrently with the measuring of the conductive flow; and estimating a property of the fluid (24) in real time during the subterranean operation based on the conductive flow and the total flow. 2. The method (60) of claim 1, wherein the fluid (24) includes an injected fluid (24) circulated through the borehole (14) from a surface location, and the constituent includes at least one of formation fluid, oil, water and formation material. 3. The method (60) of claim 2, wherein the injected fluid (24) is a drilling fluid, (24) and the formation material includes cuttings generated by interactions between a drill bit (22) and a subterranean region (16). 4. The method (60) of claim 1, wherein estimating the property includes estimating a proportion of a volume of the constituent relative to a total volume of the fluid. 5. The method (60) of claim 1, wherein measuring the conductive flow is performed by an electromagnetic flow meter configured to apply a magnetic field to the fluid, and detect a voltage generated by the fluid. 6. The method (60) of claim 5, wherein estimating the property includes determining a change in the detected voltage, and correlating the change in the detected voltage to a change in a proportion of the constituent. 7. The method (60) of claim 6, wherein the change in the detected voltage is determined relative to the total flow. 8. The method (60) of claim 1, wherein the conductive flow and the total flow are measured at a downhole location. 9. The method (60) of claim 1, wherein the conductive flow and the total flow are measured for a volume of the fluid flowing through a return fluid line at a surface location. fO. The method (60) of claim f , further characterized by automatically controlling, in real time, at least one of an operational parameter and a composition of an injected fluid based on the estimated property of the fluid. 11. A system (10) for analyzing a fluid, characterized by: an electromagnetic flow meter configured to perform a measurement of a 

conductive flow of a fluid circulating through a borehole during a subterranean operation, the conductive flow indicative of an electrically conductive constituent of the fluid; a second flow meter configured to perform a measurement of a total flow of the fluid (24) concurrently with the measurement of the conductive flow; and a processor (36) configured to estimating a property of the fluid (24) in real time during the subterranean operation based on the conductive flow and the total flow. 12. The system (10) of claim 11, wherein the fluid includes an injected fluid circulated through the borehole from a surface location, and the constituent includes at least one of formation fluid, oil, water and formation material. 13. The system (10) of claim 12, wherein the injected fluid (24) is a drilling fluid, and the formation material includes cuttings generated by interactions between a drill bit (22) and a subterranean region (16). 14. The system (10) of claim 11, wherein the processor (36) is configured to estimate the property based on estimating a proportion of a volume of the constituent relative to a total volume of the fluid (24). 15. The system (10) of claim 11, wherein the electromagnetic flow meter (42) is configured to apply a magnetic field to the fluid, and detect a voltage generated by the fluid (24).
